page: The UpCloud HICPU-8xCPU-16GB is a compute-focused virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration includes 8 vCPUs, which map to 8 physical cores with 1 thread per core, operating under a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and includes 200 GB of local SSD storage alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates a split performance profile. While single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is positioned in the bottom 10%, multi-core CPU execution and Geekbench multi-core scores fall within the middle 50% average. Memory performance shows notable constraints, with large-block write bandwidth and PassMark memory scores ranking in the bottom 10%, and uncached large-block read bandwidth in the bottom 25%. However, cached small-block read bandwidth remains average. Computational tasks like Gzip compression, Bzip3 decompression, and Clang software compilation consistently achieve average tier results.

Given its shared CPU allocation and modest memory-to-core ratio, this server offers a cost-efficient entry point for multi-threaded workloads that do not require dedicated CPU pinning or intensive memory write operations. The resource profile is best suited for multi-threaded batch processing, software compilation, medium-scale web serving, and lightweight LLM inference tasks using smaller models, where multi-core scaling offsets the lower single-core and memory write speeds.
description: The UpCloud HICPU-8xCPU-16GB is an x86_64 virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor, featuring an AMD EPYC 7542 processor clocked at 2.0 GHz. It provides 8 vCPUs under a shared CPU allocation model, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM and 200 GB of local SSD storage. Performance benchmarks place its multi-core processing, software compilation, and cryptography capabilities in the average tier, though single-core performance and uncached memory write bandwidth are constrained to the bottom 10%. This configuration represents a budget-friendly option for compute-centric workloads that benefit from multi-threaded execution but do not demand sustained, dedicated CPU performance or high memory throughput. It is suitable for multi-threaded batch jobs, software building, and moderate web serving.
og_description: UpCloud HICPU-8xCPU-16GB is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 200 GB SSD storage, offering balanced multi-core performance for compute-focused workloads.

tagline: A shared-core virtual server combining eight AMD EPYC cores with local SSD storage for budget-conscious multi-threaded processing.

16.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
200 GB of local SSD storage included
Average multi-core CPU and software compilation performance
Constrained single-core and memory write speeds
Best suited for multi-threaded batch processing and web serving
